title: CBDT Clarifies GAAR Grandfathering for Pre-2017 Investments
content:
# CBDT Clarifies GAAR Grandfathering for Pre-2017 Investments

Ek important update hai! The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) ne General Anti-Avoidance Rules (GAAR) ke grandfathering provisions ko lekar clarity announce ki hai. This is a significant move for taxpayers, especially concerning investments made before April 1, 2017.

Here are the key points from the recent notifications:

*   **Notification Details:** CBDT issued Notification No. 54/2026 and Notification No. 55/2026, both dated March 31, 2026.
*   **Clarification on Pre-2017 Investments:** The main clarification states that GAAR provisions **nahi apply honge** (will not apply) to any income derived from the transfer of investments that were made by a person before April 1, 2017. This brings much-needed relief and certainty for legacy investments.
*   **Applicability of GAAR:** While GAAR will apply to arrangements from which a tax benefit is obtained on or after April 1, 2017, this specific exemption for income from pre-April 2017 investment transfers still holds.
*   **Rules Amended:** Notification No. 54/2026 amends Rule 10U of the Income-tax Rules, 1962 (applicable to the erstwhile Income Tax Act, 1961), and Notification No. 55/2026 amends the comparable Rule 128 of the Income-tax Rules, 2026 (applicable to the new Income Tax Act, 2025). The amendments to ITR 1962 are effective from March 31, 2026, and those for ITR 2026 are effective from April 1, 2026.

This clarification reinforces the government's intent to protect genuine pre-2017 investments from GAAR scrutiny, ensuring a clearer tax landscape.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal/latest-news/notifications)

*Published on: 5 April 2026*
